content.problemStatement:
You are given an array of NNN integers and an integer KKK.

Your task is to determine the number of subarrays whose sum is divisible by KKK.

Formally, count the number of pairs (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) such that

(AL+AL+1+‚ãØ+AR)‚Äämod‚ÄäK=0(A_L + A_{L+1} + \dots + A_R) \bmod K = 0(AL‚Äã+AL+1‚Äã+‚ãØ+AR‚Äã)modK=0for 1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N.


Input Format

The first line contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.


Output Format
Print a single integer, the number of subarrays whose sum is divisible by KKK.

code.Python3:
import sys

data=sys.stdin.read().split()
N=int(data[0])
K=int(data[1])

arr=list(map(int,data[2:]))

freq=[0]*K
freq[0]=1

prefix=0
ans=0

for x in arr:
    prefix=(prefix+x)%K
    ans+=freq[prefix]
    freq[prefix]+=1

print(ans)
